author: omerfirmak
content: of course, if the guest is making use of the pub_keys it should validate them against the signature (unlike zevm). I am just saying that I would prefer guests be able to ignore hints (in general, not just public_keys) if they don't really need them. but if you need guests to validate the hints as well (even if they don't use them), they have no other choice but to do that.

author: omerfirmak
content: I am just thinking out loud, I might be overlooking things.

author: jsign
content:
I think if we are including perf hints at the spec level is because we know all guest programs must use them (e.g. in pubkeys keys should have an ~20% proving perf enhancement impact) -- so would be weird someone just ignores them 😅  I think if we reach that conclusion, I would rather prefer remove `pub_keys` or any perf hint that isn't properly justified. 

The hints add overhead, complexity, more test cases to cover coherency of some sort, etc.

author: jsign
content:
> (e.g. in pubkeys keys should have an ~20% proving perf enhancement impact)
we can re-challenge this at some point -- but this was the conclusion about a year ago. more than happy if we conclude the perf improvement isn't required and we can simplify.
